description: A flowerless plant with feathery fronds that unfurl from spiral fiddleheads. 'Phero' names the primordial elegance—plants older than flowers, reproducing by spore, their fronds encoding the spiral mathematics of growth.
sound_symbolism: The word unfurls gently. 'Phe' /ɸe/ begins with soft primordial breath, ancient presence. Then 'ro' reaches outward, the frond extending from its spiral center.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo phero' (the fern). Takes the vegetal classifier 'lipha' when counted. Associated with shade, moisture, forest floors, and ancient lineage.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Fern fronds were among Art Nouveau's most beloved motifs—the spiral fiddlehead and feathered frond embodying organic mathematics.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Ferns marked ancient forests and offered fiddleheads as spring food—plants from before flowers, carrying deep time in their form.
